title: Does the cyclic group C_645 admit a (645,161,40)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: The packet is human-ready on bounded publication audit: Baumert-Gordon 2004 still anchors (645,161,40) as a residual Table 3 row, the bounded exact/alternate-notation passes did not reveal a later settlement, and the proof artifacts preserve a short structural nonexistence argument. Lean is now a secondary archival seal, not a prerequisite for publication status.
micro_paper_assessment: The packet passes the strict micro-paper lane. It closes a named residual Baumert-Gordon Table 3 row with a short structural nonexistence proof, bounded publication audit did not uncover rediscovery, and the remaining work is short-note packaging plus optional Lean archival sealing.
family_affinity: Strong multiplier-plus-contraction fit inside the cyclic residual-row lane, with the decisive step coming from the prime factor 5 where the multiplier acts trivially.
generalization_signal: Moderate: the translate-killing step modulo a prime factor of v should transfer, but the final C15 coefficient obstruction is row-specific.
strongest_honest_claim: The cyclic group C_645 does not admit a (645,161,40) difference set. Within the bounded publication audit, Baumert-Gordon 2004 still anchors the exact row as a residual Table 3 case, no later direct settlement surfaced, and the preserved proof artifacts give a short structural disproof via the 11-multiplier, the mod-5 translation kill, the forced mod-3 contraction (61,50,50), and the exact C5-to-C15 zero-refinement obstruction.
paper_title_hint: Nonexistence of a cyclic (645,161,40) difference set
candidate_theorem_slice: Using Baumert-Gordon Theorem 3.2 with w=645 and t=11, one obtains 11D = D + s. Reducing modulo 5 forces s ≡ 0 (mod 5), so after translation one may assume 11D = D. For that normalized set, the mod-3 contraction is exactly (61,50,50), and no mod-5 quotient profile refines to an 11-orbit-constant mod-15 coefficient vector with the forced sums and square-sum.
next_action: This packet is HUMAN_READY and has been moved to LEAN_QUEUE for formal sealing; do not block fresh curation/solve work on pending Lean.
